name: Micro Precheck
when_to_use: Immediately after PRIME exits and before TEACH. | When readiness is unknown or likely drifted.
when_not_to_use: Never before PRIME for first exposure. | Skip only when emergency time constraints force direct review mode.
required_outputs: CalibrateItemResults (per-item correctness, latency, confidence) | ReadinessSnapshot (accuracy summary) | CalibrationGapSummary (predicted vs actual gap) | DominantErrorSeed (highest-frequency error class) | MicroCalibrateSnapshot (opening readiness signal for TEACH routing)
primary_citations: Kornell et al. (2009) | Roediger and Karpicke (2006)

name: Full Calibrate Probes
when_to_use: After TEACH close artifact is produced. | Before ENCODE method selection and retrieval scheduling.
when_not_to_use: Never as a replacement for opening MICRO-CALIBRATE. | Skip only when a validated full-calibrate result already exists for the same objective scope.
required_outputs: FullCalibrateItemResults (per-item correctness, latency, confidence) | ConfidenceTaggedResults (per-item confidence + correctness) | HighConfidenceMisses (overconfidence items) | LowConfidenceHits (underconfidence items) | CalibrationRiskFlags (routing signals for downstream methods) | FullCalibrateSnapshot (post-TEACH diagnostic summary)

name: Full Calibrate Priority Set
when_to_use: After M-CAL-002 completes. | Before selecting ENCODE methods.
when_not_to_use: If fewer than 3 valid items exist; run micro-recalibration first.
required_outputs: FullCalibratePrioritySet (exactly 3 ranked weaknesses) | WeaknessScoreTable (scoring breakdown per weakness) | EncodeRoutingSeed (one ENCODE method-family recommendation per weakness) | FullCalibrateHandoff (diagnostic-to-encode handoff contract)
primary_citations: Rawson and Dunlosky (2011) | Bjork and Bjork (2011)

name: Story Confidence Tag
when_to_use: After a narrative PRIME or TEACH story chunk | When learner prefers top-down big-picture processing | As an optional supplement before FULL CALIBRATE probes
when_not_to_use: As the only calibrate method in a first-exposure chain | After FULL CALIBRATE has already been completed for the same scope | When material has no narrative structure
required_outputs: Story confidence rating (1-3 scale) | Fuzzy zones list (narrative breakdown points) | Priority encoding targets (focus areas for ENCODE phase) | Optional probe seed suggestions for M-CAL-002

name: KWIK Hook
required_outputs: KWIK hook (sound-function-image triplet) | Session log entry
tags: mnemonic | kwik | hook | gated | sop-core | dual-coding

name: Seed-Lock Generation
required_outputs: Learner-generated Seed (primary hook attempt) | Final locked hook | Generation success flag (self vs AI-assisted)

name: Draw-Label
required_outputs: Completed labeled diagram | Gap list (what was missing) | Accuracy percentage

name: Teach-Back
required_outputs: Verbal explanation (recorded or noted) | Breakdown points list | Targeted review areas

name: Why-Chain
required_outputs: Why-chain document (3-5 linked explanations) | Depth level reached | Verification status

name: Self-Explanation Protocol
required_outputs: Self-explanation notes or think-aloud log (per paragraph) | Inference gap list | Confusion points flagged | Comprehension checkpoints
tags: self-explanation | comprehension | causal-reasoning | metacognition | verbal | evidence-based

id_runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests ENCODE, but runtime control_stage is TEACH.
name: Mechanism Trace
required_outputs: Complete mechanism chain (trigger to outcome) | Because statements for each step | Branch points identified | Verification status

name: Concept Map
outputs_summary: Concept map (nodes + labeled links); Mermaid code
required_outputs: Learner-generated explanation or structured encoding artifact | Concept map with nodes and labeled links

name: Comparison Table
required_outputs: Completed comparison table | Discriminating features highlighted | Differential rules (1-2 per comparison) | Error list from initial attempt

name: Process Flowchart
required_outputs: Completed flowchart | Decision point list | Loop/feedback points identified

name: Clinical Decision Tree
required_outputs: Clinical decision tree | Red flag list | Confirmatory test per diagnosis

name: Memory Palace
required_outputs: Mental palace with placed items | Retrieval accuracy check | Weak links identified

name: Chain Linking
required_outputs: Bizarre story narrative | Retrieval success rate | Weak links (breaks in chain)

name: Hand-Draw Map
when_to_use: After receiving a structural overview (H1 map or advance organizer) | When spatial positioning aids recall (anatomy, pathways, hierarchies) | First exposure to new material with clear structure
when_not_to_use: Material is purely procedural or sequential (use flowchart instead) | Time is severely constrained (<15 min total session) | Learner has already hand-drawn this topic recently
required_outputs: Hand-drawn spatial mind map (physical artifact, confirmed by learner) | Spatial recall confidence (can learner see layout with eyes closed?) | Time spent confirmation (within 5-minute cap)
tags: spatial | hand-draw | mind-map | visual | encoding | own-words
primary_citations: Wammes et al. (2016) | Fan (2023) | Van der Weel & Van der Meer (2024)

id_runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.
name: Analogy Bridge
required_outputs: Analogy statement (A is to B as X is to Y) | Mapping table (3+ correspondences) | Breakdown points (where analogy fails and what that teaches)

id_runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.
name: Clinical Application
required_outputs: Clinical application narrative with specific patient characteristics | Presentation, testing, and intervention summary (all three addressed) | Verification notes (errors flagged against source material)

id_runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.
name: Cross-Topic Link
required_outputs: Cross-topic connection list (2-3 connections) | Explanatory sentences (each with a "because" clause) | Shared principles identified for each connection

id_runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.
name: Side-by-Side Comparison
required_outputs: Side-by-side comparison table (5-7 rows minimum) | Discriminating features highlighted (the key differentiators) | Error list from initial memory-first attempt

id_runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.
name: Case Walkthrough
outputs_summary: Case walkthrough narrative; Assessment plan; Reasoning verification
required_outputs: LearnerCaseResponse (attempt-first reasoning) | ErrorTags (typed classification of errors) | ConfidenceTag (learner self-assessment) | CorrectiveFeedback (post-attempt only) | NextTargetedProbe (follow-up question targeting identified gaps)

id_runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.
name: Illness Script Builder
required_outputs: Complete illness script (enabling conditions, pathophysiological fault, clinical consequences) | Causal chain diagram (enabling -> fault -> consequences) | Comparison to textbook version (gaps identified) | Discriminating features vs. similar conditions

name: Exit Ticket
required_outputs: Exit ticket (all 3 questions answered) | Key takeaways list (3-5 specific items) | Muddy points or weak anchors identified | Next action commitment (specific and actionable) | Questions for next session

name: Anki Card Draft
required_outputs: 3-5 drafted Anki cards in canonical CARD block format | Each card tagged by topic and source | Cards staged in Brain for Anki sync | Card count logged to session record

name: Drill Sheet Builder
required_outputs: DrillSheet (30-60 interleaved items, objective-tagged) | CrossSessionValidation checklist (two-session pass criteria)

name: Post-Learn Brain Dump
when_to_use: End of any learning session as closure activity | After encoding + retrieval blocks to consolidate | When learner wants metacognitive summary of what stuck vs. what didn't
when_not_to_use: Very short sessions (<15 min) where Exit Ticket suffices | Pure drill/speed sessions where Exit Ticket is better fit
required_outputs: Brain dump text (raw recall output) | Gap list (what was missing or wrong) | Gap-fill annotations (one-line corrections) | Session retention estimate (percentage of material recalled)
tags: recall | brain-dump | metacognition | gap-detection | closure | session-wrap

id_runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests PRIME, but runtime control_stage is CALIBRATE.
name: Brain Dump
outputs_summary: Connection refresh list; known-knowns snapshot; flagged gaps
when_to_use: Learner is returning to a topic with existing notes. | Tutor needs a fast re-orientation pass before ENCODE/REFERENCE.
when_not_to_use: Brand-new topic with no prior notes (route to M-PRE-010 then M-PRE-008). | High-anxiety learner who reads this as a test.
required_outputs: StrongConnections list | MissingNodes list | FollowUpTargets list

name: Overarching Pre-Question Set
when_to_use: New topic where conceptual framing is unclear. | Learner benefits from curiosity hooks before details.
when_not_to_use: Learner shows high anxiety with open-ended prompts. | Session time is too short to process questions meaningfully.
required_outputs: PreQuestionSet (3-5 broad conceptual prompts that jointly cover the full selected scope) | PriorityPrompts (top 1-2 prompts for downstream use) | FollowUpTargets list

id_runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests PRIME, but runtime control_stage is CALIBRATE.
name: Prior Knowledge Scan
required_outputs: Connection map (topic + related concepts with labeled links) | Primary anchoring schema identified | Prerequisite gap list

name: Hierarchical Advance Organizer
when_to_use: New, dense, or mechanism-heavy topics. | Learners who prefer big-picture-first orientation.
when_not_to_use: Learner already demonstrates expert-level schema familiarity. | Ultra-familiar topics where organizer adds redundancy.
required_outputs: PillarTree (3-5 pillars, max depth 2, collectively covering the full selected scope) | PriorKnowledgeLinks (one bridge per pillar) | StructuralHypotheses

name: Skeleton Concept Hierarchy
when_to_use: Big-picture orientation for integrative topics. | Learners who think visually and need category scaffolding.
when_not_to_use: Pure procedural skill acquisition where step flow is primary. | Sessions with <3 minutes available.
required_outputs: SkeletonMap (central topic + 4-6 categories + descriptors that collectively cover the full selected scope) | CategoryLabels list | CrossLinks list

name: Structural Skimming + Pillar Mapping
when_to_use: Slide decks, textbooks, lecture packets. | Learners who need fast structure before details.
when_not_to_use: Highly spatial content where diagram-first review is required. | Sessions with fewer than 3 minutes total.
required_outputs: PillarMap (3-5 pillars with structural descriptors that collectively cover the full selected scope) | ObjectiveLinks (pillar-to-objective mapping) | FollowUpTargets list

id_runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests PRIME, but runtime control_stage is CALIBRATE.
name: Pre-Test
required_outputs: Pre-test answers with confidence ratings for each item | Calibration baseline (accuracy vs confidence alignment) | Encoding priming record (questions kept visible for study phase)

name: Structural Extraction
when_to_use: Dense or complex modules where the learner needs a high-signal schema first. | Medical/STEM/exam-prep content with many interacting concepts.
when_not_to_use: Very short sessions where M-PRE-010 alone is sufficient. | Ultra-simple fact lists better handled with direct objective framing.
required_outputs: StructuralSpine (node count within node_cap, high-signal nodes only) | Objective linkage map (every node linked to at least one objective) | UnknownNodeList (orientation gaps, not scores) | PriorityNodes list

name: Syntopical Big-Picture Synthesis
when_to_use: Multi-source study packets (slides + summary + glossary). | Clinical reasoning or research-heavy topics that need integration.
when_not_to_use: Single-source content where cross-source synthesis adds no value. | Time budgets below 3 minutes.
required_outputs: UnifiedTopDownTree (integrated hierarchy from all sources) | CrossSourceLinks list | ConflictFlags list | FollowUpTargets list

name: Learning Objectives Primer
outputs_summary: ModuleObjectiveMap or FocusObjectiveCard; selected focus objective; learner acknowledgment
when_to_use: Session start for any new module/topic. | Re-entry after long gap where objective state must be re-anchored.
when_not_to_use: Never skip for first exposure; this is the mandatory PRIME gate.
required_outputs: ModuleObjectiveMap or FocusObjectiveCard (based on scope) | SelectedFocusObjectiveId | LearnerAcknowledgment

name: Terminology Pretraining
outputs_summary: TerminologySet; abbreviation map; concise component definitions
when_to_use: Dense terminology, abbreviations, or nomenclature would otherwise make TEACH hard to follow. | First exposure to anatomy, neuro, or systems content with many named parts.
when_not_to_use: Terminology load is already light and obvious from the source. | The learner already has a stable working vocabulary for this topic.
required_outputs: TerminologySet | AbbreviationMap | ComponentDefinitionList

name: Big-Picture Orientation Summary
when_to_use: The learner needs a quick big-picture frame before details. | The material has a clear storyline, sequence, or purpose that can be summarized at a high level.
when_not_to_use: The learner is already using a stronger structural method and does not need an additional overview. | The requested move is really explanation-first TEACH rather than PRIME orientation.
required_outputs: NorthStarSentence | OrientationSummary | MajorSectionList

name: Ambiguity and Blind-Spot Scan
when_to_use: The source is dense, compressed, or likely to skip reasoning steps. | The learner wants PRIME to surface where TEACH will need to slow down.
when_not_to_use: The source is already very explicit and low ambiguity. | The requested move is learner diagnosis rather than source-gap scanning.
required_outputs: AmbiguityLog | UnsupportedJumpList | FollowUpTargets
primary_citations: Overoye et al. (2021) | Pan and Rivers (2023) | Hausman and Rhodes (2018)

name: Error Autopsy
required_outputs: Completed error autopsy table (all 4 columns for every error) | Root cause analysis for each error (Column 3 filled with specific conceptual confusion) | Discrimination cues created (Column 4 with actionable prevention strategy) | Anki card candidates identified from high-value errors

name: Mastery Loop
required_outputs: Mastery achieved on all targeted items (or partial progress with time noted) | Loop count per item (difficulty metric) | Weak anchor list (items needing 3+ loops) | Updated spacing recommendations based on loop performance

name: One-Page Anchor
required_outputs: One-Page Anchor (definitions, decision rules, examples, near-misses, traps — all present) | Trap list and near-miss set (3 canonical + 3 near-miss examples minimum)

name: Question Bank Seed
required_outputs: Question Bank Seed (10-20 items, each mode-tagged and objective-tagged) | Coverage check draft (confirming all objectives have at least one question)

name: Timed Brain Dump
required_outputs: Free recall dump with gaps marked | Accuracy assessment (correct vs incorrect items) | Gap list (missing items identified) | RSR percentage estimate

name: Sprint Quiz
required_outputs: RSR percentage (correct/total) | List of missed questions with correct answers | Weak anchor flags (uncertain but correct items) | Topics needing re-encoding

name: Fill-in-Blank
required_outputs: Completed cloze exercise | Accuracy count (correct/total blanks) | Miss list for targeted review | Second-pass improvement rate

name: Mixed Practice
required_outputs: Overall accuracy across all topics | Per-topic accuracy breakdown | Confusion matrix (which topics got mixed up) | Discrimination insights (cues that distinguish topics)

name: Variable Retrieval
required_outputs: Three retrieval outputs (free recall, application, compare/contrast) | Accuracy assessment for each format | Difficulty ranking of formats | Flexibility gap identification

name: Adversarial Drill
required_outputs: Adversarial near-miss results with discrimination accuracy | ErrorLog updates (error_type and fix_applied per miss)

name: Timed Sprint Sets
required_outputs: Timed sprint result set with per-item accuracy | Latency log (time_to_answer per item) | ErrorLog updates (misses and speed failures tagged)

name: Story Spine
required_outputs: Story spine | Ordered step list | Clinical breakpoint | Source-backed mechanism restatement

name: Confusable Contrast Teach
required_outputs: Shared bucket statement | Key difference statement | Signature clues | Classic trap | Mini application contrast

name: Clinical Anchor Mini-Case
required_outputs: Mini-case scene | Concept-to-case link | Clinical significance statement | Overgeneralization boundary

name: Modality Switch
required_outputs: Concept type | Chosen modality | Re-expressed teaching chunk | Modality rationale

name: Jingle / Rhyme Hook
required_outputs: Sequence statement | Jingle or rhyme hook | Hook-to-sequence map | Distortion warning

name: Depth Ladder (4-10-HS-PT)
required_outputs: Depth route plan | L0 hook | L3 mechanism | Fallback scaffold use or skip statement | Function confirmation | L4 precision restatement

name: KWIK Lite
required_outputs: KWIK Lite seed | Learner ownership action | Final hook | Distortion guard
